speaker: Sela
text: Sela crouches in the shadow of the aqueduct, counting the patrol torches.

speaker: Narrator
text: Two guards trade complaints about the cold; neither looks toward the wall.

speaker: Bram
text: Bram whispers about opening the north gate once the patrol passes — just a plan for now.

speaker: Sela
text: "Not yet," Sela hisses. "If we're seen, the whole watch comes down on us."

speaker: Narrator
text: A cart rattles by; a drunk sings about a gate of gold in some faraway city.

speaker: Guard
text: "Did you hear something?" The guard peers into the dark, then shrugs and moves on.

speaker: Bram
text: Bram imagines the gate swinging wide, then shakes off the daydream and waits.

speaker: Narrator
text: The patrol rounds the corner at last, torchlight fading down the lane.

speaker: Sela
text: Sela darts forward, throws the heavy bolt, and hauls the north gate open on silent, greased hinges.

speaker: Narrator
text: No cry goes up. The watch suspects nothing as the crew slips through.
